title: Terrestrial protected areas (% of total land area)
definition: Terrestrial protected areas are totally or partially protected areas of at least 1,000 hectares that are designated by national authorities as scientific reserves with limited public access, national parks, natural monuments, nature reserves or wildlife sanctuaries, protected landscapes, and areas managed mainly for sustainable use. Marine areas, unclassified areas, littoral (intertidal) areas, and sites protected under local or provincial law are excluded.

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include New Caledonia, Venezuela, RB, Bhutan, while the lightest include Eritrea, Somalia, Fed. Rep., Libya; that contrast is the fastest way to read the protected land pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Protected land share shows where terrestrial conservation designations cover a larger part of the country.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Protected land and seas can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Protected seas lives in the same category, so it can feel plausible until the high and low countries are checked.
Women, Business and the Law index overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Environment maps reward separating land cover, exposure, and emissions intensity.
Check both forested and arid or industrialized outliers.

reviewNotes: Conservation-designation map broadens the environmental catalog.
Readable enough for Daily if the generated map has enough variation.
